quick_facts.label: CBSE role definition
quick_facts.value: For CBSE-affiliated secondary and senior secondary schools, the January 19, 2026 amendment distinguishes a Career Counsellor from a Counselling & Wellness Teacher (Socio-Emotional Counsellor).

quick_facts.label: School staffing context
quick_facts.value: CBSE’s amended Clause 2.4.12.2 requires one Career Counsellor for up to every 500 students enrolled in Classes IX–XII, maintaining a counsellor-to-student ratio of 1:500.

quick_facts.label: Relevant education route
quick_facts.value: For the CBSE Career Counsellor role, the stated qualification is a bachelor’s or master’s degree in Humanities, Science, Social Sciences, Management, Education, or Technology. A B.Ed. is therefore relevant as an education-degree route, but candidates must also meet the role’s competency and capacity-building expectations.

quick_facts.label: Core professional focus
quick_facts.value: CBSE identifies career-assessment interpretation, knowledge of higher education in India and globally, research and study skills, student and parent career counselling, and collaboration with universities and industry as Career Counsellor competencies.

sections.heading: What an Educational Counsellor does
paragraphs: In the school-based career-counselling context, the role supports informed education and career decisions: interpreting career assessments, helping students understand higher-education options in India and abroad, building research and study skills, engaging parents in career counselling, and collaborating with universities and industry. CBSE treats these as competencies for its Career Counsellor designation.

A National Career Service (NCS) education-sector vacancy illustrates the broader student-support remit commonly attached to counselling roles: individual and group sessions on academic, career, personal and social issues; career-interest guidance; coordination with schools on skill-development initiatives; workshops on careers, skills and mental-health awareness; and record keeping. This is an employer listing, rather than a universal statutory job description.
bullets: Use assessment results and conversations to guide education and career planning.
Provide individual and group guidance to students; involve parents where appropriate.
Research pathways, courses, admissions ecosystems and career options, then communicate them clearly.
Build links with higher-education institutions, employers and other relevant partners.
Maintain appropriate counselling records and run information sessions or workshops when required by the employer.

sections.heading: Entry routes from B.Ed. and related qualifications
paragraphs: Requirements are employer- and board-specific; there is not one single India-wide entry qualification for every job titled “Educational Counsellor.” For CBSE-affiliated schools, the January 2026 standard for a Career Counsellor accepts a bachelor’s or master’s degree in Education, among several other disciplines. This makes B.Ed. a relevant academic route for the node, though applicants should check the vacancy and applicable board or state rules.

CBSE also requires the appointed Career Counsellor to have the listed career-counselling competencies and to complete 50 hours of Board-preferred capacity-building programmes for career counselling. Where a Career Counsellor is unavailable, a school may nominate a trained teacher, who must acquire or complete the specified qualifications and competencies within two academic years.

For professionals seeking empanelment on the Ministry of Labour and Employment’s NCS platform as a counsellor, NCS lists routes including a relevant postgraduate degree in Psychology, Social Work, Education, Counselling, Child Development or Special Education with counselling experience, or a graduate/postgraduate degree plus a recognised Diploma in Guidance and Counselling, subject to the platform’s eligibility conditions.
bullets: Complete B.Ed. or another relevant bachelor’s/master’s qualification accepted by the target employer or school board.
Develop demonstrable competence in career assessment interpretation, higher-education guidance, research skills and parent counselling.
Undertake the CBSE-preferred 50-hour career-counselling capacity-building requirement when pursuing the CBSE school role.
Consider a recognised guidance-and-counselling diploma and supervised or documented counselling experience, particularly where an employer or NCS empanelment route asks for them.
Read each recruitment notice carefully: school-board, state-government and private-employer criteria can differ.

sections.heading: Skills and professional capabilities
paragraphs: The strongest role-specific skill set combines career-information literacy with counselling practice. CBSE explicitly calls for competence in career-assessment interpretation; knowledge of Indian and global higher education; research and study skills; student and parent career counselling; and collaboration with universities and industry.

Where a role also covers socio-emotional counselling, CBSE separately identifies social-emotional-learning delivery, crisis intervention, identification of mental-health concerns, parent/teacher sensitisation and confidentiality as relevant competencies for the Counselling & Wellness Teacher designation. Applicants should distinguish this socio-emotional role from the separately defined Career Counsellor role when reading vacancies.
bullets: Career and aptitude-assessment interpretation.
Researching and comparing education and career pathways.
Clear, age-appropriate communication with students and families.
Ethical handling of sensitive information and appropriate confidentiality practices.
Partnership-building with colleges, universities, employers and school staff.
Workshop facilitation and career-information presentation.

sections.heading: Where the role is performed
paragraphs: A principal setting is the secondary or senior secondary school. Under CBSE’s January 2026 amendment, CBSE-affiliated schools are to appoint Career Counsellors for students in Classes IX–XII at the stated 1:500 ratio. In practice, the day-to-day work may include scheduled counselling, parent interactions, assessment interpretation, career sessions and coordination with external education and industry partners.

Career guidance can also be delivered through public employment and career-service infrastructure or remotely. NCS states that counsellors may provide services face to face or through telephone, video conferencing, web/portal or third-party collaboration interfaces, and may coordinate with Career Centers to schedule sessions.
bullets: CBSE-affiliated secondary and senior secondary schools.
Other school systems and education organisations, subject to their own recruitment rules.
Career Centers and employment-guidance settings.
Online or hybrid counselling arrangements where the service and employer permit them.

sections.heading: Professional development and progression
paragraphs: There is no single nationally prescribed promotion ladder for Educational Counsellors. Progression commonly depends on the employer, demonstrated counselling experience, additional qualifications, responsibility for a larger student cohort, and specialisation in career guidance or socio-emotional support.

One formal, platform-specific development option is NCS empanelment. NCS distinguishes a “Counsellor” from a “Vocational Guidance Expert”: its published eligibility table gives the latter category a postgraduate degree plus at least five years of relevant counselling/vocational-guidance experience, or a graduate degree plus at least ten years. This is an NCS platform category, not a universal job promotion rule.
bullets: Build documented counselling experience and a portfolio of workshops, pathway research and student-support work.
Add guidance-and-counselling training or a relevant postgraduate qualification where required or valued by the target employer.
Develop a specialism, such as higher-education guidance, career assessment, employability, or socio-emotional student support.
Consider NCS counsellor or vocational-guidance-expert empanelment if its eligibility criteria match your experience.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: Pay is highly variable by school type, city, funding model, workload, qualifications and whether the role combines career guidance with wider student-wellbeing duties. As dated context only, an NCS-listed, now-expired education-sector vacancy for a “Counselling and Career Development Officer” advertised ₹25,000–₹28,000 per month, was posted on July 6, 2024, and listed 0–1 years of experience. It should not be treated as a current market rate, pay norm or guarantee.
bullets: Check the salary, employment status, teaching or administrative load, student caseload and benefits in the specific vacancy.
Treat historical job-listing pay as an example of one employer’s offer, not as a benchmark for all Educational Counsellor roles.
